docs.title: TargetFinder Demo
docs.text:
This website presents short demonstrations of several target selection techniques developed around TargetFinder.

The goal is to provide a simple public-facing demo page with videos, installation instructions, and runnable examples.

docs.title: What is shown here?
docs.text:
• Target detection with TargetFinder
  • Bubble Cursor
  • DynaSpot
  • Semantic Pointing
  • Ninja Cursors

docs.title: Link with TargetFinder
docs.text:
This demo project does not duplicate the source code of TargetFinder.

Instead, it explicitly imports target-finder-toolkit as a dependency. This keeps the demo connected to the main toolkit, so improvements in the main project can be reused by this demo repository.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • macOS, Windows, or Linux/X11
  • A webcam for Ninja Cursors
  • Camera permission for gaze-based techniques
  • Accessibility/Input Monitoring permission may be required on macOS
  • X11 is recommended on Linux. Wayland is not fully supported for cursor hiding and click redirection.

docs.title: Clone the demo repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/xinqikiki/targetfinder-demo.git
cd targetfinder-demo

docs.title: macOS / Linux
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install --upgrade pip

docs.title: Windows PowerShell
docs.text:
python -m venv .venv
.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
python -m pip install --upgrade pip

docs.title: Install the demo dependencies
docs.text:
pip install -e .

This installs target-finder-toolkit from the main TargetFinder repository. The demo repository does not duplicate the toolkit code.

docs.title: TargetFinder
docs.text: python examples/run_targetfinder.py

docs.title: Bubble Cursor
docs.text: python examples/run_bubble.py

docs.title: DynaSpot
docs.text: python examples/run_dynaspot.py

docs.title: Semantic Pointing
docs.text: python examples/run_semantic.py

docs.title: Ninja Cursors
docs.text:
python examples/run_ninja.py

Ninja Cursors requires a webcam and may need camera permission before it can track gaze.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• On macOS, allow Terminal or your Python app in Accessibility and Input Monitoring if cursor control or click redirection does not work.
  • On Windows, run commands from PowerShell inside the activated virtual environment.
  • On Linux, use an X11 session when possible. Wayland may block cursor hiding or synthetic click behavior.

docs.title: TargetFinder
docs.text: TargetFinder detects interactive targets on the screen and displays their bounding boxes.

docs.title: Bubble Cursor
docs.text: Bubble Cursor expands the effective selection area around the nearest target. The user can click even if the pointer is not exactly inside the target, as long as the bubble selects it.

docs.title: DynaSpot
docs.text: DynaSpot dynamically changes the activation area around the pointer according to movement speed. The spot grows during fast movement and shrinks when the pointer slows down.

docs.title: Semantic Pointing
docs.text: Semantic Pointing modifies pointer behavior near detected targets to make selection easier.

docs.title: Ninja Cursors
docs.text: Ninja Cursors uses gaze to select one cursor among eight distributed cursors. The selected cursor can then be used to click the intended target.

docs.title: Platform Support
docs.text:
The code includes platform-specific handling for macOS, Windows, and Linux/X11.

  • macOS: uses Quartz and ApplicationServices for click redirection and cursor handling.
  • Windows: uses Windows APIs for cursor and mouse settings.
  • Linux: targets X11, using X11/XFixes and xinput where needed.

Wayland is not fully supported for cursor hiding and click redirection.

docs.title: Videos
docs.text: This page presents short demonstrations of the TargetFinder-based interaction techniques.
